previous_statement: (Multi-constraint induction — Weis-Shirokov Corollary 5). Under m constraints C = {x ∈ K : f_j(x) ≤ β_j (or = β_j), j = 1,...,m}: dim(F_K(x)) ≤ dim(F_C(x)) + m. Proof: Apply the single-constraint result iteratively. Define K_0 = K, K_1 = {x ∈ K_0 : f_1(x) ≤ β_1}, ..., K_m = C. At each step: dim(F_{K_{i-1}}(x)) ≤ dim(F_{K_i}(x)) + 1. Summing (telescoping): dim(F_K(x)) = dim(F_{K_0}(x)) ≤ dim(F_{K_m}(x)) + m = dim(F_C(x)) + m.
new_statement: (Multi-constraint induction — Weis-Shirokov Corollary 5, amended). Under m constraints C = {x ∈ K : f_j(x) ≤ β_j (or = β_j), j = 1,...,m}: dim(F_K(x)) ≤ dim(F_C(x)) + m. Proof: Apply Node 1.2.1 (single-constraint case) iteratively. Define K_0 = K and for j = 1,...,m, define K_j = {x ∈ K_{j-1} : f_j(x) ≤ β_j} (for inequality constraints) or K_j = {x ∈ K_{j-1} : f_j(x) = β_j} (for equality constraints). Then C = K_m. By Node 1.2.1 applied at each step: dim(F_{K_{j-1}}(x)) ≤ dim(F_{K_j}(x)) + 1. Summing (telescoping): dim(F_K(x)) = dim(F_{K_0}(x)) ≤ dim(F_{K_m}(x)) + m = dim(F_C(x)) + m. Note: each constraint costs at most 1 dimension whether it is an equality or inequality, since Node 1.2.1 handles both level and sublevel sets. This is a deduction by iteration from Node 1.2.1.
